content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
August
18,
1993
Thomas S.
Knox
Knox,
Lemmon,
Brady,
Anapoisky
& Sheridan
300 Capitol Mall,
Suite 1170
Sacramento,
CA 95814
RE:
Your Request for
Confirmation of Telephone Advice
Our File No.
A—93-285
Dear Mr.
Knox:
You have requested confirmation of telephone advice provided
to you on behalf of the members of the California—Nevada Super
Speed Ground Transportation Commission
(the
“SST Commission”)
concerning the conflict of interest disclosure provisions of the
Political Reform Act.l/
Your letter dated July 21,
1993,
correctly summarizes my
advice that the
SST Commissioners will not be required to file
leaving office statements of economic interests
(Form 730)
unless
legislation re—authorizing the
SST Commission
is defeated or
vetoed.
You indicated that you expect the legislation to be
considered and approved sometime in late August.
Therefore, if
approved,
the
SST Commissioners will continue to file annual
statements of economic interests no later than April
1 each year.
Please contact me at
(916)
322-5662 if you have any questions
regarding this matter.
Sincerely,
Wayne Ordos
Executive Director
3
By:
Alice Hughes
Political Reform Consultant
1/
Government Code Sections
81000—91015.
All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000,
et g.
All references to regulations are to Title
2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
42S J Sireet, Suite SOO
•
P.O. Box 807
•
Sacramento, CA
95804-0807
•
(916) 322-5660

sections.question_synthetic: Whether members of the California-Nevada Super Speed Ground Transportation Commission must file Form 730 (leaving office statements of economic interests) upon the expiration or defeat of re-authorizing legislation.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: SST Commissioners will not be required to file Form 730 leaving office statements unless the re-authorizing legislation is defeated or vetoed. If legislation is approved as expected, commissioners will continue filing annual statements of economic interests by April 1 each year.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a confirmation letter of prior telephone advice with no explicit QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections. The advice is provided in narrative form in the body letter. Document references Political Reform Act provisions (Government Code Sections 81000-91015).

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether members of the California-Nevada Super Speed Ground Transportation Commission must file Form 730 (leaving office statements of economic interests) upon the expiration or defeat of re-authorizing legislation.

CONCLUSION: SST Commissioners will not be required to file Form 730 leaving office statements unless the re-authorizing legislation is defeated or vetoed. If legislation is approved as expected, commissioners will continue filing annual statements of economic interests by April 1 each year.
embedding.first_500_words: California Fair Political Practices Commission August 18, 1993 Thomas S. Knox Knox, Lemmon, Brady, Anapoisky & Sheridan 300 Capitol Mall, Suite 1170 Sacramento, CA 95814 RE: Your Request for Confirmation of Telephone Advice Our File No. A—93-285 Dear Mr. Knox: You have requested confirmation of telephone advice provided to you on behalf of the members of the California—Nevada Super Speed Ground Transportation Commission (the “SST Commission”) concerning the conflict of interest disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act.l/ Your letter dated July 21, 1993, correctly summarizes my advice that the SST Commissioners will not be required to file leaving office statements of economic interests (Form 730) unless legislation re—authorizing the SST Commission is defeated or vetoed. You indicated that you expect the legislation to be considered and approved sometime in late August. Therefore, if approved, the SST Commissioners will continue to file annual statements of economic interests no later than April 1 each year. Please contact me at (916) 322-5662 if you have any questions regarding this matter. Sincerely, Wayne Ordos Executive Director 3 By: Alice Hughes Political Reform Consultant 1/ Government Code Sections 81000—91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et g. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 42S J Sireet, Suite SOO • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660
embedding.summary: The FPPC confirms telephone advice regarding whether SST Commission members must file Form 730 statements, concluding they will not need to file leaving office statements if re-authorizing legislation is approved, but will continue annual filings.
